name: Featured Launchpad List
description: Curated list or grid of featured blueprints, archetypes, or starter paths. Each item explains who it is for, what it scaffolds, and the next command or registry route a builder should use.

curated.description: Three to six featured launchpad cards with blueprint name, target workflow, short outcome copy, tag row, and primary inspect/use action.

slots.item-header: Blueprint or launchpad name with namespace badge.
slots.item-outcome: One concise sentence describing what the builder can ship.
slots.item-tags: Small tags for workflow, target framework, or adoption mode.
slots.item-command: Optional install or scaffold command snippet.
slots.item-action: Inspect, launch, or copy command action.

visual_brief: A curated launchpad surface that feels selective and high-signal. Cards should read like strong product recommendations, not a generic content feed. Each item needs a clear use case, a reason to trust it, and an obvious next step. Use compact tags and command previews sparingly so the list remains scannable.

responsive.mobile: Single-column cards with full-width action rows and command snippets that wrap without overflow.
responsive.tablet: Two-column card grid with equal-height cards and aligned action rows.
responsive.desktop: Three-column grid or split featured-plus-secondary layout. Keep the primary featured item visually stronger only when the content warrants it.

layout_hints.curation: Prefer fewer, better launchpad entries over a dense catalog wall. The pattern should communicate recommendation quality.
layout_hints.command_preview: Use command snippets only when they accelerate action. They should be copyable or visually separate from prose.
layout_hints.metadata: Tags should explain fit: greenfield, brownfield, hybrid, Next, Vite, registry, dashboard, or SaaS.

motion.micro: Cards lift subtly and action rows sharpen on hover or focus.
motion.transitions: Featured items can reveal with a short stagger when the list first enters view.

keyboard: Tab order should move through each card's primary action in reading order.
announcements: Each launchpad should expose name, outcome, and target workflow without relying on tag color.
accessibility.focus_management: If the whole card is clickable, still expose one named primary link or button.

composition.LaunchpadCard: Card > [Header(Name + NamespaceBadge) + Outcome + TagRow + CommandPreview? + ActionRow]
